title: Student Loan Fund remittance due April 16
desc: The Student Loan Fund (SLF) has asked employers who began deducting loan repayments from employees' salaries in March to remit the funds via the e-PaySLF system by April 16.

title: Cooling centres get the thumbs up
desc: As temperatures surge across Bangkok, stepping indoors is no longer a matter of comfort but, for many, a necessity.

title: Energy minister will not side with 'oil hoarders'
desc: Energy Minister Akanat Promphan warns the government will take tough action against oil hoarding, adding he has never bowed to business interests and that anyone found stockpiling fuel will face severe legal consequences, even if they are wealthy.

title: Fuel prices cut before Songkran
desc: Fuel supplies will remain adequate throughout the Songkran holiday, while retail oil prices were set to fall from Saturday after the Oil Fuel Fund Executive Committee approved cuts to levy rates, Energy Minister Akanat Promphan said.

title: Aerothai picks 2,500 flights per day
desc: Flight volumes during the Songkran holiday are expected to average more than 2,500 flights per day, with traffic remaining concentrated at major airports and key tourist destinations, says Aeronautical Radio of Thailand (Aerothai).

title: Thailand not ready for border talks with Cambodia: Sihasak
desc: Thailand is not ready to attend the Thailand-Cambodia Joint Boundary Commission (JBC) meeting scheduled for April 17-25, as internal procedures remain incomplete, Foreign Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow said on Saturday.

title: Review seeks 'quality' tourists
desc: The government is reviewing its visa-free entry scheme for foreign visitors, aiming to revise the policy to better target higher-quality tourism, Tourism and Sports Minister Surasak Phancharoenworakul said.

title: Cabinet approves B7.7-billion relief package
desc: The cabinet has approved a 7.7-billion-baht economic relief package to support vulnerable groups, businesses, the transport sector and farmers in response to the economic impact of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.

title: PM vows to turn all policies into actions
desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul says he has accepted recommendations about his government’s policy statement and will push forward all announced measures to ensure maximum benefit for the public, stressing that the government would complete its full four-year term.

title: What are Thailand’s visa changes really about?
desc: There have been a number of announcements from state agencies to do with visa policies in Thailand over the past several months that seem to have made the matter rather convoluted.

title: Twenty road fatalities on first ‘dangerous day’
desc: Twenty people died and 132 were injured in 135 road accidents across Thailand on Friday — the first of the “seven dangerous days” of Songkran holiday travel, the Department of Disaster Prevention and Mitigation said on Saturday.

title: 泰国批准逾77亿泰铢救助民生与行业补贴
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月11日，泰国总理府发言人拉差妲宣布，当天内阁会议已通过一项总额超过77.42亿泰铢的中央预算拨款方案。该预算主要分为三大部分：约60.22亿泰铢用于照顾弱势群体，约14.58亿泰铢支持运输业及商用司机，约2.606亿泰铢用于缓解民众生活成本压力。

title: 阿努廷重划八大任务组 亲自负责反腐禁毒与跨国犯罪打击
desc: 泰国头条新闻社 据泰媒报道，4月11日，泰国总理兼内政部长阿努廷签署总理府命令，决定将国家行政管理按任务组划分，由各任务组负责人统筹推进相关领域工作。

title: 宋干节航班高峰将至 泰国国内航班仍为主力
desc: 泰国头条新闻社 据泰媒报道，4月10日，泰国航空无线电有限公司表示，预计2026年4月10日至19日宋干节期间泰国境内航班日均约2562架次，集中在主要机场及旅游城市。

title: 在泰德籍男子接单攻击他人网络被撤销居留许可
desc: 泰国头条新闻社 据泰媒报道，4月10日，泰国移民局在曼谷Thong Lo地区抓获一名嫌疑涉嫌跨国网络犯罪的26岁德国籍男子，并撤销其在泰居留许可。

title: Longer-term impacts from war constrain SET
desc: The Stock Exchange of Thailand saw big swings during March, especially at the beginning of the month, after Israel and the United States started bombing Iran on the last day of February.

title: Man found dead in remote Phatthalung forest with bullet wound
desc: Man found dead in remote Phatthalung forest with bullet wound Legacy A 29 year old man was found dead in a remote forest above Khlong Hua Chang Reservoir in Phatthalung province...

title: 泰国财政部拟推300亿低息贷款支持能源转型
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月10日，泰国副总理兼财政部部长艾尼提透露，财政部计划于4月11日提交一系列紧急措施给内阁审议...

title: 曼谷ICONSIAM宋干节庆典盛大开幕
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月10日，泰国曼谷知名地标ICONSIAM携手公共和私营部门，在湄南河畔共同举办了一场超大规模的宋干节庆典...

title: Thai economy braces for stagflation amid Iran war: How real estate must pivot to survive
desc: Thai economy braces for stagflation amid Iran war: How real estate must pivot to survive Legacy The secretary-general of the National Economic and Social Development Council ( NESDC ), Danucha Pichayanan, said that the prolonged conflict and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz for over a month had significantly impacted global energy market confidence. Iran has proposed negotiation conditions, such as the withdrawal of US military bases from the Middle East. This marks an initial stance for talks with the US and Israel. Whether an agreement will be reached remains unclear. Global crude and ref
